description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant, cylindrical in shape with a domed top and a hexagonal operating nut at the center of the dome. The hydrant features three prominent hose connection outlets: two on opposite sides and one facing forward, each capped with a round, bolted cover. The body is robust, with reinforced flanges at the top and bottom, and the surface displays a textured, slightly weathered finish typical of cast iron hydrants exposed to the elements. The hydrant is painted a vivid red, a common color for high-visibility emergency equipment in urban environments. No chains are visible connecting the caps, and there are no discernible manufacturer markings or logos on the surface. The object is designed to be fixed in place, typically installed adjacent to curbs or sidewalks for easy access by emergency personnel.
description: A robust, red cylindrical fire hydrant with three capped hose outlets and a domed top, featuring a textured, weathered surface. It is designed for fixed installation in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central hose outlet and operating nut, with both side outlets visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the right-side hose outlet and the domed top, with the front outlet partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hydrant with both side outlets visible, but no central outlet.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the left-side hose outlet and the domed top, with the front outlet partially visible.
